title: Vladimir author Julia May Jonas: ‘We’re imprisoned by our obsessions’
description:
As her debut novel becomes a Netflix series starring Rachel Weisz and Leo Woodall, the American author talks about comparisons with Lolita, moving on from #MeToo and problematic authors

When we meet in a cafe near her Brooklyn apartment, three weeks before the TV adaptation of her debut novel Vladimir hits Netflix, Julia May Jonas is feeling an anticipat
hook: A novelist obsessed with desire and morality watches her work become a Netflix series—confronting what it means when fiction about transgression goes mainstream.

title: Prayer
description: The fallout of Blue Moon is an assertive, pile-driving movie star performance from Ethan Hawke, but most compelling is how Richard Linklater and his collaborators have rendered this occasion to tell about a life—even a night’s life—in the terms one uses to speak about art itself. Which is just living and dying by another name. Which is what a song does.
hook: Linklater films a single night as if it were art itself—Ethan Hawke's performance becomes a meditation on how we narrate our own lives.

title: How China Learned to Love the Classics
description: The Chinese Communist Party has embraced the study of Greek and Latin—as, in some ways, an antidote to the modern West.
hook: China's Communist Party is teaching Plato and Virgil as ideological armor against the West—a sharp reversal of who gets to claim the Western canon.
